extra.message: User input from the 'name' parameter is directly interpolated into the SQL string using Python string formatting (% operator) on line 42-43. This allows SQL injection attacks. The query is constructed as: q = ("INSERT INTO students (name) VALUES ('%(name)s')" % {'name': name}), where 'name' comes directly from user input (request.post data) without any escaping or parameterized query usage.

Jinja2 template autoescape is explicitly disabled with 'autoescape=False' on line 35. This disables HTML escaping for all template variables, allowing XSS attacks when user-controlled data is rendered in templates without explicit escaping.

User-controlled data (student name) is rendered without escaping on line 16: {{ name }}. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows XSS attacks if a student name contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.

User-controlled data (course title) is rendered without escaping on line 17: {{ course.title }}. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows XSS attacks if course title contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.

User-controlled data (course description) is rendered without escaping on line 18: {{ course.description }}. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows XSS attacks if course description contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.

User-controlled data (course title) is rendered without escaping on line 14: {{ course.title }}. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows XSS attacks if course title contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.

User-controlled data (course description) is rendered without escaping on line 15: {{ course.description }}. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows XSS attacks if course description contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.

User-controlled data (review text) is rendered without escaping on line 22: {{ review.review_text }}. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows XSS attacks if a review contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.

User-controlled data (course title) is rendered without escaping on line 19: {{ course.title }}. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows XSS attacks if course title contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.

User-controlled data (course description) is rendered without escaping on line 20: {{ course.description }}. With autoescape disabled in app.py, this allows XSS attacks if course description contains malicious HTML/JavaScript.

The review() endpoint (lines 111-131) does not require authentication. Any unauthenticated user can POST to /courses/{course_id}/review and submit reviews without being logged in. The @authorize() decorator is missing from this function, unlike the logout() function which has it.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The student() endpoint (lines 64-80) does not verify authorization. The function retrieves a student by ID from user-supplied input (request.match_info['id']) on line 66 without checking if the requesting user has permission to view that specific student. This is an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability - any unauthenticated user can view details of any student by guessing or enumerating student IDs.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
